level_signals: 3+ years of experience in growth, product growth, or growth engineering
Expected to operate with high autonomy and own end-to-end product surface area

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Bachelor's degree or higher in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related technical field
3+ years of experience in growth, product growth, or growth engineering at a SaaS or fintech company
PM brain, engineer hands: You can mock up an MVP and then go build it yourself, scoping what matters, why, and shipping it without waiting for someone else to spec it
Production AI experience: You've built and shipped agents in production,not theoretical systems, but real ones that ran autonomously, handled edge cases, and got better over time

Builder mentality: Demonstrated ability to spec, build, and ship product experiences, not just manage them; comfortable writing code, reviewing PRs, and working in a codebase alongside eng and product teams

Deep AI fluency: Hands-on experience with modern AI coding harnesses (Cursor, Claude Code, Codex); you've prototyped, shipped, and iterated on AI tools, agents, and workflows, not just managed roadmaps about them
Operational empathy: You can partner with sales, finance, risk, and other business teams to deeply understand their challenges and deliver rapid AI-powered solutions
High ownership: You communicate clearly, own your work completely, and don't wait to be told what to build next — comfortable with autonomy and ambiguity

scope.surface: Partner channel growth — partner-facing product experiences, PLG experiments, internal growth tooling, and AI agents for partner workflows
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS / fintech — channel partner-led growth contributing to enterprise revenue
core_responsibilities: Deploy Product-Led Growth to the Partner Channel: Design and ship PLG experiments targeted at partner-sourced leads from banners to in-product referral triggers

Build Partner-Facing Product Experiences: Own the end-to-end deployment and iteration of partner experiences including incentives, landing pages, demos, badging, MDF, partner resources, and gamification mechanics that drive referral behavior from partners
Partner Sell-Through Experiences: Own the full suite of experiences that improve partners' ability to sell Ramp from discovery to referral.
Partner Assets: Ship products that enable partners to access marketing assets, product information, and co-branded materials
Partner Platform Growth Experiments: Design and ship new growth experiments to increase referral volume and deepen partner engagement.

Cross-Functional Partnership: Partner closely with sales, data, finance, and engineering teams to scope, prioritize, and ship partner growth features; serve as the product owner for the partner growth surface area
Build Growth Acceleration Tooling: Build partner-facing tools that make partner sellers more effective and internal-facing tooling to scale marketing campaigns faster
Build AI Agents from Scratch: Build autonomous agents that run partner workflows end-to-end.

green_flags: Highly specific role surface area (partner growth) — not a vague generalist posting
Explicitly expects the hire to write code and work in codebases alongside engineers — signals genuine technical autonomy
AI-first mandate is clearly articulated with specific tools named (Cursor, Claude Code, Codex)
Relocation support to NYC or SF mentioned, signaling willingness to invest in the hire
Competitive benefits package including 401(k) match, full medical/dental/vision, and parental leave
Unlimited AI token usage as a perk aligns with the AI-first role mandate
red_flags: Location type is ambiguous — relocation to NYC or SF mentioned, but no explicit remote/hybrid/onsite policy stated in role body
High bar: requires both engineering fluency (writing code, reviewing PRs) AND PM-level product ownership simultaneously — a rare combination
No explicit years threshold beyond the 3+ minimum despite 'Senior' title — seniority expectations may be higher in practice

location.type (inferred as unclear; relocation to NYC/SF mentioned but no explicit onsite/hybrid/remote policy in JD body)

ambiguities: Location policy is not stated. The JD mentions 'Relocation support to NYC or SF (as needed)' in benefits, implying in-office presence may be expected, but no explicit onsite/hybrid/remote designation appears in the role body. Marked as 'unclear'.
The role archetype blends Growth PM, Growth Engineer, and Technical PM — 'Growth (non-PM)' selected as closest fit but the role has significant PM and engineering expectations.
